tools.name: axiorank_score_tool_call
tools.description: Inspect a proposed agent tool call BEFORE executing it. AxioRank scores its risk (0–100), runs content inspection (secrets, PII, destructive ops, prompt-injection, egress), applies your policies, records an audit log, and returns a decision: `allow`, `deny`, or `hold`. On `hold`, a human must approv

tools.name: axiorank_verify_card
tools.description: Vet a remote agent or tool's identity card (A2A Agent Card, MCP server card, OAuth metadata, x402, …) BEFORE connecting to it. Supply EITHER `url` (AxioRank fetches the card) OR an inline `document`. AxioRank verifies signatures, scores supply-chain risk, folds in cross-tenant threat intel, and retu

tools.name: axiorank_check_approval
tools.description: Poll the verdict of a held (`hold`) tool call. Pass the `approvalId` returned by `axiorank_score_tool_call`. Blocks briefly server-side and returns as soon as an operator approves/denies; otherwise returns the still-`pending` status so you can call again. Requires the `gateway:write` scope and the s

tools.name: axiorank_get_protocol_coverage
tools.description: List the agent-interop protocols AxioRank can govern (A2A, MCP, OAuth, x402, DIDs, robots.txt/llms.txt, AP2, …), grouped into six planes, each with coverage status (live/beta/planned) and direction (inbound/outbound/both). Use this to discover what AxioRank speaks before wiring up card verification

tools.name: axiorank_get_health
tools.description: Liveness/readiness probe for the AxioRank control plane (API + database reachability). Returns `status: ok|degraded`.

tools.name: axiorank_list_agents
tools.description: List the active agents in your AxioRank workspace (id, name, labels, quarantine + last-used status). Use this to find an agent's id before calling agent-specific tools. Requires the `agents:read` scope.

tools.name: axiorank_get_agent
tools.description: Fetch one agent's posture (quarantine state, labels, last-used, revocation) by id, scoped to your workspace. Requires the `agents:read` scope.

tools.name: axiorank_quarantine_agent
tools.description: Reversible kill switch: quarantine an agent so the gateway DENIES all of its tool calls, or release it. Set `quarantine: false` to restore. Requires the `agents:write` scope.

tools.name: axiorank_revoke_agent
tools.description: Permanently revoke an agent: its API keys stop authenticating immediately (incident response). Irreversible. Issue a new agent to restore access. Its audit history is kept. Requires the `agents:write` scope. Prefer `axiorank_quarantine_agent` for a reversible pause.

tools.name: axiorank_list_policies
tools.description: List the outbound governance policies in your workspace (enabled or not), each with its tool pattern, action (allow/deny/require_approval/redact), risk threshold, priority and enabled flag. Requires the `policies:read` scope.

tools.name: axiorank_get_policy
tools.description: Fetch one outbound policy by id, scoped to your workspace. Requires the `policies:read` scope.

tools.name: axiorank_create_policy
tools.description: Create an outbound governance policy. It is created DISABLED so an LLM can never arm enforcement unattended. Review it (axiorank_get_policy) then enable it with axiorank_update_policy. `toolPattern` is a glob over tool names; `action` is allow / deny / require_approval / redact (redact masks PII in

tools.name: axiorank_update_policy
tools.description: Update an existing policy: enable/disable it, rename it, or change its priority. (Other fields are edited in the dashboard.) Requires the `policies:write` scope.

tools.name: axiorank_search_audit_logs
tools.description: Search your workspace's governance audit trail, newest first, with filters: decision (allow/deny/hold), source (sdk/mcp), agentId, tool name (substring), minimum risk, and a time window (ISO `from`/`to`). Payloads are already secret-redacted. Use it to answer questions like "show denied tool calls i

tools.name: axiorank_list_incidents
tools.description: List security alerts/incidents in your workspace, newest first, filterable by status (open/acknowledged/resolved/suppressed), severity (low/medium/high/critical) and kind (high_risk/anomaly/auto_response/kill_chain/ml_threat). Requires the `logs:read` scope.

tools.name: axiorank_get_incident
tools.description: Fetch the kill-chain finding behind an alert (the multi-step attack pattern, severity, and the contributing tool-call ids) by alert id. Returns null evidence for a non-kill-chain alert. Requires the `logs:read` scope.

tools.name: axiorank_list_threat_intel
tools.description: List external identities (card hosts/keys) flagged across the AxioRank network, with k-anonymity-gated aggregate counts (workspaces, sightings, deny/review, max risk). This is the shared cross-tenant feed, not your private data. Requires the `logs:read` scope.

tools.name: axiorank_list_ml_assessments
tools.description: List your workspace's most recent ML/LLM threat assessments (model verdict, threat class, confidence, recommendation) for tool calls and cards. Requires the `logs:read` scope.

tools.name: axiorank_get_usage
tools.description: Report this billing period's usage for your workspace: plan tier, governed events used vs the monthly limit, and ML assessments used. Requires the `logs:read` scope.

tools.name: axiorank_issue_token
tools.description: Mint a short-lived, scoped `axr_tok_…` access token for an existing agent. This is the Zero-Trust credential the agent sends as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. It expires within the hour and cannot be replayed afterwards. Durable static keys are issued in the dashboard, not here. Requires the `keys

tools.name: axiorank_create_agent
tools.description: Create a new agent in your workspace and return a SHORT-LIVED bootstrap token to start using it immediately (no durable secret is emitted). For long-term auth, add a static key or a federation binding in the dashboard. Requires the `agents:write` scope.

tools.name: axiorank_author_detector
tools.description: Describe a risk in plain English and AxioRank's AI proposes a custom content detector (regex or keyword) and SAVES it DISABLED for your review. An LLM never arms detection unattended. Outbound content categories only (secret/pii/destructive/injection/egress). Requires the `policies:write` scope and

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "axiorank_score_tool_call" references sensitive artifacts (secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Inspect a proposed agent tool call BEFORE executing it. AxioRank scores its risk (0–100), runs content inspection (secrets, PII, destructive ops, prompt-injecti…

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "axiorank_list_agents" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: List the active agents in your AxioRank workspace (id, name, labels, quarantine + last-used status). Use this to find an agent's id before calling agent-specifi…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.
